All right, welcome back. So this video is going to be a little bit longer than the others, but in this video I'm literally tactically going to show you specifically what to do to get money right now. So the first foremost most obvious thing is if you had promotions that worked before and you can run them again to your list, then run them again to your list. Like it really is that simple. I can't tell you how many times we've done that for clients and just seen huge gains because they're so distracted by doing other stuff that they never think to just run the dang thing again and then turn it evergreen. All right, so that's the obvious. Now what might not be so obvious are unique situations like well what if you had something that worked before but it was time sensitive or you want to run it a certain way or what if you have an offer that you've always been running and you don't have any anything really that's particularly performed better than or worse than anything else you're doing. You've only ever done this one thing or something like that. So that's where I want to get really granular with you in this video. So one thing we'll do for clients and that we also do here is let's say we'll take a sales process that has worked and let's say it's a webinar and we ran the webinar and the webinar worked really really well. Well what we'll do is instead of running the webinar again and making everybody on the list like go register for the webinar we will essentially use diagnostics which will go into a lot in much greater detail during the certification and we'll use diagnostics and determine that in almost every single circumstance the webinar itself really isn't the thing that actually gets the sales. The webinar itself is the thing that gets someone to behaviorally indicate that they're interested in buying and that the real money is in actually what happens when you follow up with the people who watch the webinar. So we will then create a new campaign using email framework some about to share with you. So whether or not we're talking about a webinar or sending people to a sales letter or sending people to a video sales letter or sending people to an article or whatever essentially what I want to do for you in this video is show you the main email campaign structures that we always use to create campaigns from scratch for people often times using existing assets like okay you have a sales letter that worked let's build an email campaign and an automated sequence to drive people to that sales page for example. So in this particular video I'm going to show you the big ones which are the click sequence and the countdown sequence and I'm going to show you how to use those in conjunction with one another. Alright so I'm going to give you the frameworks and the process maps of how to do this and of course I'm also going to give you the frameworks and process maps for what to say and how to automate all of this stuff. So without further ado let's get to work. This is one that if you have anything for sale right now and you have any kind of database you can use that I'm showing you in this video to go out and get sales from your database like right now. Alright so let's get you some money. Let's go. Guess what three types of them period and on the planet there's three types now we could like try to pretend and church it up and be like well there's the story sequence and there's the yada and that's bullshit. There's three types of email sequences in the world. Number one there's transactional receipts and stuff right. It could also be if you wanted to say like an update really hey just filling you in you know a barber got a new cat the other day yada yada but generally speaking there's transactional receipts and stuff like that that and apply to us we don't mess with that then you got a quick sequence right so in like this process right here. Okay the go back the go finish that's just a click sequence that's all that is. You got transactional click sequence and then you got a deadline sequence and sometimes you hear me talk about a countdown sequence it's the same thing I like to use various words to describe the exact same thing so that's it so in terms of our universe we really only have two click sequences and deadline sequences so let's talk about the click sequence usually a click sequence the framework for it it's two emails a day for three days that's it whole purpose of the click sequence is to get the reader to go do something you know that's it like go click on this thing so hey I made this video for you go watch it happens to be a sales video but you'll find that out when you go see it or hey go register for this webinar or hey I got this new book go look at it and buy it like that's all we're doing is telling them to go do something that's a click sequence and usually you send a couple emails a day for three days and that's it and there's no hard and fast rule here you know do it three emails a day for three days if you want to it doesn't matter and most cases by the way and so in like all of these sales processes you're going to see and dang near every single one of them if they click but they don't do what you want them to do so they go to the sales letter and they don't buy for example then they're put into the other type of sequence which is a deadline sequence all right so really you know if you start thinking about it all of these sales processes in most cases every sales process you're going to see is made up of quick sequences that drives someone to one of the four possible types of webpages and ultimately they're going to go into a deadline sequence and all we're doing is just rearranging how that's you know laid out in the steps of the sales process days and I've always used it and it always worked I have no idea five days is better or three days it kind of created four-day cash machine campaign actually I didn't create that my cousin Trace Smith created that and I just popularized it and back he neither of us ever bothered longer or shorter than four days so kind of that's it you know standard issue there and here's the deal on the last day of a deadline sequence it's got six emails on every other day it's got two emails that's it so doesn't matter if it's a stack where you're doing them and it's just two days that means on day one two emails on the last day six emails if you're doing a four-day deadline sequence like classic four-day cash machine two emails a day on days one through three six emails on the last day so what's the normal to have higher click-through rates and higher clicks in your first emails and your last one because if they click on the first one they ain't gonna get any of these other ones so you're just sequences into the ground here but I'm hopefully demystifying this process for you there's just a series of emails from different angles trying to get someone to go click the same dang thing all right cool so ain't that hard right I mean it's literally it's just a matter of doing it but I'm telling you most people they'll barely do this and then they go straight to a nurture sequence or something that nobody pays any attention to so let's say you send out you know a quick sequence and it does its job and a whole lot of people go and click and they watch your sales video or they read your sales letter or whatever you know what that does not mean it does not mean that those people are going to necessarily buy as a matter of fact most of them won't but it does mean that they are interested in the results of whatever the content is that you sent them to so if you said hey I wrote this book for you and you know gardening here go take a look at it and they click over to it and they don't buy it doesn't mean they're not interested in gardening it means they're normal people and they're like oh I'll buy that later just as soon as I get done keeping up with the Kardashians because you gotta stay on top of what they're doing you know so that's where the next email sequence comes in which is the deadline slash countdown sequence both same sequence just like coming up with different names for the same thing like to confuse myself even so what I want to do for you now is walk you through exactly what to say in one of those sequences and how to structure it so you can get how do you say mo money that's what you say which does not lead to mo problems by the way okay let's get to work so to kind of recap when you got yourself a click sequence that's essentially phase one of the click sequence plus countdown sequence dealio and basically you got yourself some content that makes a special offer at the end like a vsl or an on-demand class or a webinar and you know it doesn't even really have to be educational I mean you could just send them to hey go buy this thing and then if they click it now you want to look and see what happens so it could be a straight sale a discount added bonus payment plan whatever doesn't matter essentially though like we went over last week a quick sequence is basically your email in your list about something typically twice a day for three days right and so we have that click sequence framework and copy frameworks and all that stuff from last week so that's phase one right now again like we went over last week if they click the video or whatever they're no longer in the quick sequence right and instead we move them to the next phase which is to see if they bought and if they didn't buy then we put them in the count countdown sequence of course that's what really we're going to spend most of our time on today and incidentally if they never click then we put them into a different click sequence for something else generally speaking right but if they do click well we want to see if they bought because some of them will you know you never know logic dictates most of them won't otherwise your conversion rate would be amazing and that's why we take the people the the people who clicked and we put them up in a countdown sequence right and so it's obviously related to the offer and so favorite thing ever is of course a good old-fashioned deadline based email sequence this sends them straight to the pitch and if you really want to do it right you use scarcity and your reference to deadline and your emails and on your web pages um I like to use deadline funnels for mine it works great and um you can use scarcity around a discount you can use scarcity around bonuses you can make the entire offer expire altogether um you can even if you want to use scarcity and making the like on-demand class itself available so let's say someone opts in to watch that decision-making system that I just walk you through you could if you wanted to in your own marketing if they don't get to the part where they see the pitch they're in a little deadline sequence says hey this thing's going away in the next four hours so if you want if you want this class you need to go finish it and you could actually make it go away so there's all kinds of you know uses really for a countdown sequence but generally speaking uh it kind of works like this regardless of the business type right so we start out with a quick sequence and we're sending a couple emails a day every day generally three days and then we look to see if they clicked and if they did and they didn't buy then we got them in that countdown sequence right and I have four day deadline here but as I'm about to show you you can really experiment with the layout of this thing so four day deadline is generally a couple emails a day until the last day and that's when you send a bunch of emails so whether it's a two-day deadline you know whatever the the majority of your email is focused on the final day of that deadline and then we look to see if they bought and if they didn't click or they didn't buy then we put them in something else and usually it's another combination of this making the same offer at a different angle or a new product or whatever so let's talk about some timing for this thing I used to always do it for four days and I pulled four days out of the air like remember four day cash machine and all of that never tested anything other than four days lately I'd been doing them for two days typically speaking and I'll string them together so I have a two-day countdown sequence then I'll wait today and then I'll have another two-day countdown sequence where I add an additional bonus and then I'll wait a day and then I'll have another two-day countdown sequence where I'll like offer a payment plan or something like that that works but you can really do like however you want to do it right the main thing is is that you use these things because they're good so generally speaking every day except the last day I like to get two emails out before noon and the reason I like to do it before noon is because Perry Belcher made a post and he knows what he's talking about and I believe him that according to some research most email is read between the hours of like 8 a.m. and noon don't know if he's right but hell sounded good you know so Perry's pretty smart fellow on the last day generally I send them six emails and this is assuming that the offer expires at midnight all right so it typically looks something like this 8 a.m. 10 a.m. noon then I wait forever until 9 p.m. 10 p.m. and then the final notice comes in at 11 so that's a total of six it might seem like overkill to you it would be if this was 10 years ago in today's day and age people just don't read email nearly as much as they used to so they could go you know they can miss all of these and only see these or whatever so we're just trying to get the day message in front of them here generally speaking by the way most of the sales come from these guys right here at the end this is not set in stone by the way this is just a classic framework of how to do it and how to get your clients to do it but there's a lot of ways to experiment with it and I'll show you a couple of recent experiments that I've done that sort of deviate from this just to give you the clarity that hey you can really use this however you want to as long as you freaking use it because it's really really effective you know countdown sequences are great so what do you say well stuff like this I'm like man if I followed my own frameworks I'd really do a lot better right so like as you'll see in the examples I'm about to give you I didn't follow this framework it still worked especially the closer we get to the deadline so it's usually hey your big discount or your access to this thing or whatever it ends on date and time and I like to use deadline funnel dot com it's get the point right and that is a countdown sequences are the backbone of a good marketing process the backbone of it and b it doesn't have to follow the frameworks religiously in order to work right so you could for example use this in ecom and the uh you know if they go to check out to buy the skin cleanser and they abandon it then you're offering them a bribe with the extra toner or free shipping or whatever if your clients a brick and mortar client like a barber shop they could if then they wanted to fill up the barber shop really quickly it could be like get 75% off of your haircut uh you know when you book it buy Wednesday and put them in a countdown sequence for that if you're not even selling anything but you're trying to get people on the phone it could be like hey our schedule is filling up we can only guarantee appointments through Wednesday final notice you know like doesn't really matter but people respond to deadlines and you just need to make them real so and that's why I recommend tools like deadline funnel uh kartra also has a good functionality for this uh it's not your job to do the programming it's your clients okay so that is our conclusion of how to do a countdown sequence and some examples in which countdown sequences are used if you recall from last week everything was either a click sequence or a countdown sequence in every single sales process we've looked at right now so hopefully this is starting to make stuff kind of come together for you uh nice and easy cool cool okay let's get jiggy with it i'll see you next week
